steps.title: When a fraction equals zero
steps.content: A fraction equals zero when its numerator equals zero and the denominator is nonzero. Set the numerator to zero: $(x-6)(x-8) = 0$.

steps.title: Extract the solutions and verify validity
steps.content: The equation $(x-6)(x-8) = 0$ has solutions $x = 6$ and $x = 8$. Check the denominator: when $x = 6$, we get $6 - 5 = 1 \neq 0$ ✓; when $x = 8$, we get $8 - 5 = 3 \neq 0$ ✓. Both solutions are valid.

steps.title: Sum the solutions
steps.content: Adding the two solutions: $6 + 8 = 14$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
